problem: In a small village, there are three switches, but they are not labelled. Each switch corresponds to one of three light bulbs in a room. Each light bulb is either on or off. You can turn the lights on and off as many times as you want, but you can only enter the room one time to observe the light bulbs. How can you figure out which switch corresponds to which light bulb?
reasoning_steps: Step 1: Turn switch 1 to the 'on' position for 5 minutes to heat up the corresponding light bulb.
Step 2: Turn switch 1 to the 'off' position and turn switch 2 to the 'on' position.
Step 3: Immediately enter the room to observe the light bulbs. Note the bulb that is on (switch 2) and the bulb that is off but warm (switch 1).
Step 4: Identify the remaining bulb (switch 3) as the one that is off and cold.
Step 5: Conclude which switch corresponds to which light bulb based on the observations.
final_answer: Switch 1 corresponds to the warm but off bulb, Switch 2 corresponds to the on bulb, and Switch 3 corresponds to the cold and off bulb.
